Title: Traffic Forwarding - Web traffic forwarding test
Expected Result: The web traffic should be forwarded to VIP of the proxy on the configured port.
Steps: Send the web traffic from the client side(JMeter or Ixia)

Title: Traffic Forwarding - Non-web traffic forwarding test
Expected Result: The non-web traffic should be forwarded to the VIP of CFW on the configured port.
Steps: Send the non-web from the client side(JMeter or Ixia)

Title: Traffic Forwarding - Multiple tunnel traffic forwarding test
Expected Result: The traffic should be forwarded via correct GRE tunnel.
Steps: Configure multiple tunnels and send traffic through those tunnels

Title: Traffic Forwarding - Custom port traffic forwarding test
Expected Result: The custom port traffic should be forwarded to the VIP of the proxy.
Steps: Configure a custom port and send traffic on that port.

Title: All service Validation
Expected Result: All the supervisorctl services should be in the running state.
Steps: Check the status of “supervisorctl status”

Title: Health Check - Status 200
Expected Result: The health check should return the status 200
Steps: Enable all the services behind the ECGS gateway

Title: Health Check - Status 503 in case of either CFW or proxy failure
Expected Result: The health check should return the status 503, and that tunnel should be removed from the POP
Steps: Create multiple tunnels, and one of the tunnels should pass traffic to CFW, and make the CFW disable

Title: Health Check - Status 503
Expected Result: The health check should return the status 503
Steps: The health check should return the status 503

Title: Tunnel status on all ECGW
Expected Result: All the tunnels should be present in all the ECGS gateways
Steps: Check the GRE tunnel on all the ECGW gateways

Title: Traffic stickiness for endpoints
Expected Result: All the traffic from single endpoint should land to single ECGS gateway
Steps: Take the pcap for one client IP on all the ECGW gateways

Title: POP failure test - CFW failure with no CFW feature
Expected Result: The traffic should work as normal as CFW feature is not enable for the tenant.
Steps: Shutdown the CFW and keep the proxy active, and send the traffic only through the proxy

Title: POP failure test - CFW failure with CFW feature
Expected Result: All the tenant with CFW feature should be removed from the device.(gre interface should be marked down)
Steps: Shutdown the CFW and keep the proxy active, and send the traffic for both the CFW and the proxy

Title: POP failure test - proxy failure with no proxy feature
Expected Result: The traffic should work as normal for all the tenant which are not using proxy functionality.
Steps: Shutdown the proxy and keep the CFW active, and send the traffic for CFW

Title: POP failure test - proxy failure with proxy feature
Expected Result: All the tenants using proxy feature should be removed from the node.
Steps: Shutdown the proxy and keep the CFW active, and send the traffic for both the CFW and the proxy

Title: POP failure test - proxy and CFW failure
Expected Result: All the tenant should be removed from the nodes.
Steps: Shutdown both CFW and proxy and send traffic for both

Title: Event forwarding - logs availability in nsmetric.log file and mongo db
Expected Result: The event logs and mongo db should show the same entry and have the same fields
Steps: Enable the debug mode and monitor the log file /opt/ns/log/nsmetric.log

Title: Event forwarding - Logs fields
Expected Result: It should have all the required entries with correct values.
Steps: Check the fields in the event logs

Title: Event forwarding - Check the event channel type
Expected Result: We should be using GRPC channel instead of ZMQ
Steps: Check the event channel type

Title: Probe IP - in case of CFW health check failure
Expected Result: The probe ip should stop responding if HC towards CFW fails on all nodes and cfw is marked as true
Steps: Shutdown the CFW and keep the proxy active, and monitor the prob for tenant which has cfw feature.

Title: Probe IP - in case of proxy health check failure
Expected Result: The probe ip should stop responding if HC towards Proxy fails on all nodes and proxy is marked as true
Steps: Shutdown the Proxy and keep the CFW active, and monitor the prob for tenant which has proxy feature.

Title: Probe IP - in case of both CFW and proxy health check failure
Expected Result: The probe ip should stop responding if HC towards both CFW and Proxy fails on all nodes and proxy is marked as true
Steps: Shutdown the both Proxy and CFW, and monitor the prob for tenant which has either proxy or cfw feature.

Title: Probe IP - in case of services failure on the device
Expected Result: The probe ip should stop responding if the services are in down state in all available nodes.
Steps: Change the service status as down on all nodes, and monitor the prob on any tenant.
